tweet: Nebraskan Brent Zoucha is a hero, and it is my honor to tell his story.  https://t.co/tTRXsgQeIP

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Brent’s mother remembers him as an ideal son who loved his life and everyone around him. We cannot thank him enough for his service to our country.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Brent’s death shocked the central Nebraska community, and during his service, the streets were lined with American Flags and Patriot riders.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: On June 9, 2006, Brent and four other Marines came across an IED. Tragically, all of them lost their lives due to the wounds they sustained.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: His mother remembers talking on the phone with him in early June and sending him a care package of fig cookies. She didn’t know it at the time, but that was the last conversation she would have with him. #NEheroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: This area of Iraq saw the majority of its fighting and counterinsurgency from 2004 until 2007. Brent arrived in the heat of battle. #Service

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Coincidentally, he was assigned to the exact Marine Corp unit as his older brother. Once Brent arrived to Twentynine Palms, he and his brother found out that they were both deploying to the AL An Bar Province in Iraq.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: The extreme discipline that his brother and other Marines routinely displayed greatly interested him. So after graduating from @HPCSTORM he enlisted in the @USMC. #NEheroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Like many small-town Nebraska boys, Brent spent a lot of time hunting and fishing. He had a special way with animals and even trained a chicken to fly on his shoulder on command.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Brent Zoucha grew up in the small town of Clarks, Nebraska. The youngest of four siblings, his mother described him as having a great smile and always goofing around.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: I am honored to tell Cory’s story and join Nebraskans and Americans across our county in saluting Cory’s willingness to serve.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Today, if you go to Chadron, Nebraska you will see the Sergeant Cory Mracek Memorial Post Office. I was proud to work w/ former NE Senator Mike Johanns on a bill renaming this post office in honor Cory.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Communities came together to line the streets as his funeral procession went through their town as it made its 46-mile journey across Nebraska.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: A week later 600 family members, friends, and military officials gathered inside a Nebraska gymnasium to celebrate and honor Cory and his sacrifice for our country.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Cory and two other U.S. service members were killed by the explosion. #NEhero

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: On January 27, 2004, his mother remembers spending the morning instant messaging with Cory to catch up and see how he was doing. Later that same day, Cory and his reconnaissance platoon were performing a mission where they came across an IED.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: After a couple of years in active duty, he was deployed to #Iraq to an area that saw major combat activity and sectarian violence during 2003-2007.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: He came back home in 2001 and transitioned back to the @NENationalGuard. However, when 9/11 happened, Cory told his parents that he was going to go back to the Active Army so he could serve his country. #Service

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: A year later, Cory transferred to the Active Army & was soon deployed to South Korea. He enjoyed his time there and he even participated in the #ManchuaMile where he marched for 24-miles in full combat gear.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: After graduating high school, he went on to attend college at @Chadronstate. While in college he decided to join the @NENationalGuard and left to go train at Fort Sill, Oklahoma. #NEhero

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: During high school, he became involved in many extracurricular activities and played basketball and football.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Born in Chadron, Nebraska, Cory Mracek was always trying to have a good time. He was often found either laughing or trying to make others laugh.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Watch my full Senate floor tribute to Corporal Matt Henderson who died in Iraq in May 2004 while serving our country. #NEHeroes  https://t.co/5vDtpJx7TP

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: I join Nebraskans and Americans across our country in saluting Matt’s bravery and #sacrifice. It is an honor to tell his story. #NEhero

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Matt was a consistent source of inspiration for his fellow Marines, he was the kind of young man that people were just drawn to. He was tough yet a selfless Marine. #SemperFi

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: For his ultimate sacrifice, Corporal Matt Henderson received the naval achievement medal with cluster, a Purple Heart, and he was the first to receive the prestigious Non-Commissioned Officer Combat Engineer of the Year award posthumously.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Matt warned his squad to get back and take cover but was unable to get out of range himself. The remainder of his squad survived. #Leadership #Service #Sacrifice

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: As a squad leader, Matt was aware of and concerned about the dangers of his second deployment. On May 26, 2004, Matt was leading his squad in sweeping an area in the Al Anbar province of Iraq. During the sweep, Matt and two of his men were killed when an IED was detonated #NEhero

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: In February of 2004, Matt deployed to Iraq for the second time and Jaimie moved back to #Nebraska to be closer to their families.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Upon Matt’s return home in May of 2003, he and Jaimie finally had their big church wedding in Lincoln, NE. They moved to California where Matt was stationed.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: During his deployment, Matt was chosen to be a squad leader due to his #leadership abilities & the respect of the other Marines. Matt’s family had no communication with him during this time and watched the news every evening with the hopes of catching a glimpse of Matt.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: In February of 2003, Matt deployed to Iraq where he joined other U.S. led forces in the invasion of Iraq from Kuwait in order to oust Saddam Hussein. #NEhero

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: On August 4, 2001, Matt proposed to his girlfriend Jaimie on Mission Beach in San Diego just after sunset. As they planned their wedding in Nebraska, Matt’s unit was informed they would be among the first forces to invade Iraq.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Due to his strong interest in the construction field, Matt also finished Combat Engineer School at Camp Lejeune, and he was assigned to the 1st Combat Engineers Battalion at Camp Pendleton.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: In 2000, Matt joined the @USMC because he was attracted to the discipline & direction it offered. He completed boot camp at @MCRD_SD before attending & completing infantry training at @MCIWPendletonCA. #Service

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Matt loved sports and was a devout @Huskers and @ChicagoBears fan. #GBR #Tradition

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: After Matt’s father finished veterinary school, he moved the family to the Good Life. As Matt grew up, he made many friends and could be found by his dad’s side on equine veterinary visits.

tweet: Happening Now: I am speaking on the Senate floor about the life and service of Nebraska’s Corporal Matt Henderson. #NEHeroes #Service

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: On the Senate floor today, I paid tribute to the life & service of @NENationalGuard Sgt. First Class Tricia Jameson of St. Paul, NE. Tricia will always be remembered as a #NEHero. Full video here:  https://t.co/4LUKhw3Cxa

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Sergeant First Class Tricia Jameson loved her family, she embodied the pride of her state and the values of our nation. #NEHeroes #Service 🇺🇸

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: For her service to our nation, Sergeant First Class Tricia Jameson earned many military decorations, including the Purple Heart and Bronze Star posthumously. #NEHeroes #Service

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Sgt. Jameson’s name and reputation live on as soldiers save lives, just as she did, on the battlefield.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Without hesitation, Sgt. Jameson answered the call. She was speeding towards the battlefield to attend to the fallen when her field ambulance was struck by an IED. That day, she gave her life while serving her country.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Sgt. Jameson and her battle buddy, Specialist Rachelle Spors received an urgent call to help injured Marines in combat a few miles from where they were.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: By June of 2005, Sgt. Jameson was in Iraq on duty with the 313th Medical Company at Camp Speicher. Jameson’s first mission on the road was a long one. She was the vehicle commander of an M997 ambulance that was headed to Trebil near the Jordan border.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: With her determination, she raised that program into a world-class operation. The soldiers who took her program went on to save lives around the world.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: As combat intensified in #Afghanistan and #Iraq, Tricia was tasked with improving the Combat Life Saver course, which teaches soldiers basic medical skills for the battlefield.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: After high school, Tricia joined the @NENationalGuard in 1994. With her grit and determination, she soon was promoted to become a full-time training instructor at Camp Ashland.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Tricia grew up in the farm country of St. Paul, NE, and at a young age, she advocated for what is right and how others should be treated.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Each Nebraska hero has a powerful story, and today, I’m honoring the life of @NENationalGuard Sergeant First Class Tricia Jameson.

tweet: I join Nebraskans and Americans in saluting his willingness, and his family’s sacrifice to keep us free. #NEHeroes #ProudNebraskan

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Sergeant Lonnie “Calvin” Allen, Jr. received the Bronze Star and a Purple Heart for his service to our country. #NEHeroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Bellevue also honored him by naming a street after him and his high school established the Sgt. Lonnie Calvin Allen, Jr. Scholarship.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Sergeant Allen’s memorial service was held at the Capehart Chapel in Bellevue. Over five-hundred people attended the standing-room-only ceremony to pay their respects, including over two hundred #PatriotRiders.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: During Sergeant Allen’s second deployment, patrols became increasingly dangerous. On May 18, 2006 Seargeant Allen’s Humvee was struck by an IED, which killed him and three other service members.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Shortly after their marriage, Calvin served in Iraq for the first time and Birgit moved to Bellevue to be closer to Calvin’s family while he was deployed. After a brief stint home, he deployed to Iraq for a second time. #NEHeroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Following his enlistment, Calvin soon shipped off to Fort Benning to complete his One Station Unit Training for the infantry. Shortly after graduation, he was assigned to a station in Germany. It was in Germany where Calvin met his wife, Birgit

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Although many expected he'd follow in his dad's footsteps by enlisting in the Air Force, his mother knew better: Calvin wanted to pave his own road in the Army. #NEHeroes #ProudNebraskan

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Calvin had a caring attitude, which extended to church on Sundays. He often volunteered to be an usher and displayed his musical talent in the choir. #NELeaders

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Calvin grew up in a military family. When his dad, Lonnie Sr., was assigned to @Offutt_AFB in Bellevue, he & his wife Sallie thought they would only be there for a short time. However, they enjoyed "the good life" and soon after the birth of their two sons, they decided to stay.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Chief Warrant Officer Allgaier’s memorial service was held on June 6th, at a Catholic church in Omaha. Hundreds of people, including over a hundred #PatriotRiders, turned out to pay their final respects.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: On the night of May 30, 2007, Chris flew a night operation transporting approximately 30 service members. Shortly after dropping the soldiers off for their important mission, insurgents shot down his CH-47 Chinook, killing Chris and four other service members. #NEheroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: In 2007, Chris’s unit deployed to Afghanistan. He flew CH-47 Chinook helicopters in transport missions. The Upper Sangin Valley in the Helmand Province was the center of fighting in Afghanistan in 2007.  A British newspaper called it “the deadliest area in Afghanistan.”

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Chief Warrant Officer Allgaier deployed to South Korea for over a year prior to going to Afghanistan in 2003 and Iraq in 2005.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Due to his strong academic record and interest in aeronautics, Chris was persuaded to apply for Warrant Officer Candidate School. He graduated at the top of his class and became a helicopter pilot.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Shortly after receiving his bachelor’s degree, Chris enlisted in the @USArmy. He graduated from Basic Combat Training in Fort Jackson before attending his Advanced Individual Training in aviation mechanics. #Service

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: During Chris’s senior year at Creighton Prep, he became interested in fixed wing aircraft and flying. After high school graduation, Chris continued his studies at St. Louis University, where he studied aeronautical administration.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Growing up, Chris lived a pretty typical life for a Nebraska boy. He ate at his favorite pizza place with his family, cheered on the #Huskers, and attended St. Robert Bellarmine Catholic Church in Omaha with his parents, sister, Sharon, and brother, Rob.

tweet: He returned home from combat and in February of 2007 married his childhood sweetheart, Kara, on Valentine’s Day.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Bill Bailey is a true Nebraska hero. I am honored to tell his story. #NEHeroes #ProudNebraskan

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Bill was a wonderful husband, brother, son, father. He dedicated quite a bit of his life to helping other people. #NEHeroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Specialist Bailey earned several military awards including the Purple Heart, Bronze Star, Iraqi Campaign Medal & Combat Action Badge. #Honor

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: More than one thousand people gathered in Bellevue to remember Bill. #NEHeroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: On May 25, 2007, during a security escort mission, Bill’s vehicle struck an improvised explosive device. Bill Bailey was killed. #NEHeroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: By 2007, the insurgency in Iraq had erupted to new levels of violence. Increased American forces sought to destroy the al Qaeda network.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: In 2006, his unit was deployed and assigned to provide security escorts for truck convoys operating out of Balad Air Base in #Iraq.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: That deep desire to serve the country continued to tug at him. In 2005, Bill re-enlisted in the @NENationalGuard #Courage

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Bill’s protective nature made him a great father. It also made him a great firefighter. #NeHeroes

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: After 9/11, Bill felt the call to serve his country again. But, first things first. Bill and Dee married in 2004.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: In December 2000, Bill met Dee. She was a bank teller at Wells Fargo; he drove an armored truck. They met through a service window.

name: Senator Deb Fischer
tweet: Bill attended Bellevue East where the call to protect his country prompted him to enlist in the @NENationalGuard in 1995.
